Why does a ticket stay in its current status when a requester replies? What is the expected behavior when the requester of a ticket is an agent or support person?

It is expected behavior that if a requester replies to a ticket, and is also an agent, the ticket stays in whichever status was selected at the time of their reply.

How do I create a trigger to change the ticket status to open when an agent replies as the requester? What conditions do I use to reopen tickets requested by team members?

If the requester of the ticket is an agent, make the status change to open with a trigger.

  1. Under Meet ALL of the following conditions, select Add condition:
  2. Select Object > Ticket > Ticket | Is | Updated
  3. Select Lookup relationship > Requester > Requester | Is | (current user)
  4. Select Ticket details > Current user | Is | (agent)
  5. Select Object > Ticket > Ticket status | Not changed to | Solved
  6. Under Actions, select: Object > Ticket > Ticket status | Open

 

Can agents use the Mail API to pass through a ticket status? How do I use the #open command in an email reply?

Yes. Alternatively, your agents can use the Mail API to pass through the ticket status #open as they reply by email.

articles.name: How do I access reporting and analytics?
articles.title: How do I access reporting and analytics?
articles.body:
Question

I am new and unsure where I go to open reporting and analytics. Where is Analytics located?

Answer

You can get to Analytics from anywhere in Zendesk. Click the product tray icon ( ) at the top right-hand corner of your screen. Then, select Analytics.

Troubleshooting access issues

I can't see the product tray

If you don't see the product tray, that probably means you're not logged in to Zendesk as an agent. Sign in as an agent, not end user, to get access to the agent interface.

I do see the product tray, but Analytics is not listed there

If you don't see Analytics in your product tray, make sure Analytics is included in your subscription. You may need help from the account owner for this: Viewing and managing plan subscriptions.

I can click on Analytics in the product tray but then I see "Uh oh"

If you see an error that you don't have permission to access Analytics, ask an admin to help you enable Analytics access on your agent profile in Admin Center: Error Message: "It looks like you don't have permission to access Analytics".

When I click on Analytics it takes me to an activation page

If you've never used Analytics before you need to activate it. This may take a few hours. Feel free to close the page, this will not interfere with activation.

I was given a link to a report but I can't open it

If you generally have access to Analytics but get an error when following a link to a report, check with the report owner. The owner must give you permission to view or edit: Sharing dashboards.

I can't see the Reports icon in Analytics

If your Analytics view is missing the Reports icon:

It means your current permission level only allows you to view content in Analytics. To gain the ability to create custom reports, your admin has to upgrade your account. If you want to give access to users, check this article: Giving users access to Explore.

I can't find Explore. I see Analytics instead

Zendesk rebranded Explore as Analytics. To access it, click the product tray icon () at the top right-hand corner of your screen. Then, select Analytics.

For more information, see this article: Getting started with Zendesk Explore for reporting and analytics.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I access reporting and analytics? Where is Analytics located? How do I find Explore?

Concept

You can get to Analytics or Explore from anywhere in Zendesk.

Action steps

  1. Click the product tray icon at the top right-hand corner of your screen.
  2. Select Analytics.

I can't see the product tray? Why is the product tray missing?

Scope and limitations

If you do not see the product tray, you are not logged in to Zendesk as an assignee or agent. Sign in as an assignee or agent, not a requester or end user, to get access to the agent interface or inbox.

I do see the product tray, but Analytics is not listed there? Why is Analytics missing from the product tray?

Scope and limitations

If you do not see Analytics in your product tray, make sure your plan type or subscription includes Analytics. You may need help from the account owner to view and manage plan subscriptions.

"Uh oh" It looks like you don't have permission to access Analytics? How do I fix the permission error in Analytics?

Scope and limitations

If you see an error that you do not have permission to access Analytics, ask an admin to help you enable Analytics access on your agent profile or role in Admin Center.

When I click on Analytics it takes me to an activation page? Why does Analytics show an activation page?

Scope and limitations

If you never used Analytics or Explore before, you need to activate it. This process takes a few hours. You can close the page because closing it does not interfere with activation.

I was given a link to a report but I can't open it? Why can't I open a shared report link?

Scope and limitations

If you generally have access to Analytics but get an error when you follow a link to a report, check with the report owner. The owner must give you role permissions to view or edit the sharing dashboards.

I can't see the Reports icon in Analytics? Why is the Reports icon missing in Analytics?

Scope and limitations

If your Analytics view is missing the Reports icon, your current permission level or role only allows you to view content in Analytics. To gain the ability to create custom templates or reports, your admin must upgrade your account.

articles.name: My help center displays incorrectly
articles.title: My help center displays incorrectly
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

My help center has issues with functionality or displays incorrectly. For example, buttons do not work properly, or page-elements do not align. What is the best way to troubleshoot these issues in Knowledge?

Resolution steps

The first step is to determine if the issues are from the Zendesk platform or from your custom code. Test if the behavior occurs within the default Copenhagen help center theme first.

Use the theme preview functionality on a non-active Copenhagen theme, which is the default theme in Zendesk.

To test on a default version without any impact on the live site:

  1. In Knowledge admin, navigate to the Customize design icon
  2. Select the Add theme button and choose the Add Copenhagen theme option, which will not go live
  3. Find the newly created Copenhagen theme that appears in the Theme Library and click Customize
  4. Preview the various template pages within your help center
    • To preview the theme with levels of access, select the option of your choice under Preview role
  5. Verify if the issue happens in the default Copenhagen theme
    • If the issue occurs in the default Copenhagen theme, Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help
    • If the issue doesn't occur within the Copenhagen theme, the custom code applied to your live theme most likely causes the issue. Consider either having the creator of that theme correct its code or use a different theme, for example, Copenhagen or a custom theme
    • If your updated custom theme is ready to go live, click ⋮ > Set as live theme
Disclaimer: This article is provided for instructional purposes only. Zendesk doesn't support or guarantee custom code and custom themes. Zendesk can't provide support for third-party technologies such as JavaScript, jQuery, HTML, or CSS. Reach out to the author of any code for support.

From your theme preview, be aware that the community appears even if it's off in your settings. The community won't display in the live theme if you turned it off.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

My help center displays incorrectly? Why do buttons not work properly or page-elements do not align in Guide or knowledge base? How do I troubleshoot functionality or layout issues?

Determine if the issues originate from the Zendesk platform or from your custom code. Test if the behavior occurs within the default Copenhagen help center theme first. Use the theme preview functionality on a non-active Copenhagen theme without any impact on the live site.

  1. In Knowledge admin, navigate to the Customize design icon.
  2. Select the Add theme button and choose the Add Copenhagen theme option.
  3. Find the newly created Copenhagen theme that appears in the Theme Library and click Customize.
  4. Preview the various template pages within your help center. To preview the theme with levels of access, select the option of your choice under Preview role.
  5. Verify if the issue happens in the default Copenhagen theme. If the issue occurs, Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help. If the issue does not occur, the custom code applied to your live theme causes the issue. Consider either having the creator of that theme correct its code or use a different theme. If your updated custom theme is ready to go live, click Set as live theme.

Does Zendesk support custom code or third-party technologies? Can I get help with JavaScript, jQuery, HTML, or CSS?

Zendesk does not support or guarantee custom code and custom themes. Zendesk cannot provide support for third-party technologies such as JavaScript, jQuery, HTML, or CSS. Reach out to the author of any code for support.

Does the community or Forum display in the theme preview? Why does the community appear in preview when it is off in settings?

From your theme preview, the community appears even if you turn it off in your settings. The community will not display in the live theme if you turned it off.

articles.name: Troubleshooting issues with offline messages and Chat
articles.title: Troubleshooting issues with offline messages and Chat
articles.body:
In Zendesk, use this guide if you experience any of the issues below:

  • You only receive offline messages and no live chat messages from end users
  • The widget is not online even though your agents are logged in
  • Your customers don't see the live chat option, only an option to send an offline message

This article contains the topics below.

  • Step 1: Determine if agents are online
  • Step 2: Verify your department settings
  • Step 3: Check for connectivity issues
  • Step 4: Review security Settings

Step 1: Determine if agents are online

Live chat is offered to visitors when at least one agent is online. For more information about setting your availability, see the article: Configuring your chat availability settings.

Step 2: Verify your department settings

Determine if you set the right department and staff. Consider the steps below to understand if the department is staffed correctly:

  • Check if the department has agents online
  • Confirm that agents are assigned to the created department
  • If you use the API to set departments on your website, confirm the script, and select the right department

For more information, see the articles listed below:

  • How do I set or change the department of a chat?
  • Recipe: Route a chat to a department based on the URL of the website
  • Automatically route chats to departments

Step 3: Check for connectivity issues

Review your live chat connection settings. Check if your dashboard is connected to the internet. Chat offers you a connection monitor to resolve issues related to connectivity. For more information on how to troubleshoot your connectivity, see the article: Troubleshooting your Chat connection.

Step 4: Review security Settings

Configure your widget security settings. Investigate if the right configuration was set up in your security setting. Go to Settings > Widget > Widget security and check Blocked countries and Allowed domains. For more information about the widget's security setting, see the article: Restricting the Chat widget by country or domain.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I only receive offline messages and no live chat messages from end users or visitors? Why is the widget not online even though my agents or support persons are logged in? Why don't my customers or clients see the live chat option but only see an option to send an offline message?

Live chat is offered to visitors or customers when at least one agent, support person, or team member is online. To resolve these issues, you must determine if agents are online, verify your department or team settings, check for connectivity issues, and review security settings.

Can I offer live chat to visitors or customers if all my agents or support persons are offline?

No. Live chat is offered to visitors or customers when at least one agent, support person, or team member is online.

How do I verify my department or team settings to ensure the widget displays online? How do I check if my team is staffed correctly for live chat?

Consider the steps below to verify your settings:

  1. Check if the department or team has agents online.
  2. Confirm that agents are assigned to the created department.
  3. If you use the API to set departments on your website, confirm the script, and select the right department.

How do I troubleshoot Chat connectivity issues or network problems? How do I use the connection monitor to resolve internet issues?

Review your live chat connection settings and check if your dashboard or inbox is connected to the internet. Chat offers you a connection monitor to resolve issues related to connectivity.

How do I configure widget security settings to unblock domains or countries? How do I check if widget security is blocking customers from seeing live chat?

Investigate your configuration by completing the following steps:

  1. Go to Settings Widget Widget security.
  2. Check Blocked countries and Allowed domains.

articles.name: Troubleshooting common issues with view visibility and freshness
articles.title: Troubleshooting common issues with view visibility and freshness
articles.body:
In some cases, a ticket view (sometimes named ticket queue or folder by some of our users) may not appear in your views list, or it may not display the expected tickets. Use this guide to find solutions to the most common issues with views.

The article contains the topics below:

Check your browser

This could be a cached issue. Clear the cache and cookies of your browser, try a different supported browser, a private or incognito window, or deactivate any proxy or VPN.

Confirm the group you are part of has access to the view

  1. Contact an admin of the view 

  2. Ask them to verify the view setting Who has access

  3. And to ensure that the group assigned to you in your profile matches the view setting

Ensure you are allowed to see tickets the tickets are assigned to

Ensure if tickets in that view are assigned to a specific group under Assignee and open your Zendesk profile to confirm that you are allowed to view that ticket.

Verify your custom role has access to those tickets

If you are on an Enterprise plan, ask an admin on your account to verify that the role assigned to you is allowed to view those tickets.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is a ticket view? Why does my folder or ticket queue disappear? Why can I not see my expected tickets?

A ticket view or ticket queue or folder organizes your tickets. Sometimes it does not appear in your views list or display the expected tickets due to local browser caching issues or restricted permissions.

How do I fix cached issues with my views? How do I troubleshoot a ticket queue or folder that does not display correctly?

Clear the cache and cookies of your browser. Alternatively, try a different supported browser, open a private or incognito window, or deactivate any proxy or VPN.

Can I view a ticket view or folder if my team or group does not have access? How do I confirm my group has access to a view?

You cannot see a view if your group lacks access. To fix this, contact an admin of the view and ask them to perform these steps:

  1. Verify the view setting Who has access.
  2. Ensure that the group assigned to you in your profile matches the view setting.

What plan type or pricing tier restricts role access to tickets? Can I verify permissions for my custom role on an Enterprise plan?

The Enterprise plan allows role-based ticket restrictions. If you use an Enterprise plan, ask an admin on your account to verify that the Role or access level assigned to you allows you to view those tickets.

How do I ensure I am allowed to see tickets in a view? How do I verify my assignee or support person permissions?

Ensure if tickets in that view are assigned to a specific group or department under Assignee. Then, open your Zendesk profile to confirm that you are allowed to view that ticket.

articles.name: Why is my support address not verified?
articles.title: Why is my support address not verified?
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

After adding the external support address to Zendesk Support, the email address isn't verified. I see the error message: Forwarding check failed.

Resolution steps

Verify that the forwarding rules within your email domain are set up correctly. The verification error is caused by a ping that is sent from your Support account to the linked support address. If forwarding is set up correctly, the ping returns to the account. For more information on forwarding your email to Zendesk, see the article: Forwarding incoming email to Zendesk Support.

If the forwarding is set up correctly in your account, but you receive an error for the SPF record check, make sure that there is an SPF record set up for that address.

The SPF record is not required for Zendesk to send outgoing notifications. The SPF record allows Zendesk to send emails from your domain. In the absence of an SPF record, the email will show as originating from Zendesk. For more information, see the article Allowing Zendesk to send email on behalf of your email domain. 

If the error message persists after completing the steps above, contact your email administrator or email provider for further assistance.

If you would like to reevaluate your email setup and start over, click on the button below to navigate to our in product guide.

.zendesk.com
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why is my external support address not verified? Why does my email address show "Forwarding check failed"? Why am I getting an email verification error?

This error occurs because Zendesk Support sends a ping from your account to the linked support address. If you set up your forwarding rules correctly within your email domain, the ping returns to the account and verifies the address.

How do I fix a "Forwarding check failed" error? How do I verify my external support email address?

  1. Verify that the forwarding rules within your email domain are set up correctly.
  2. Contact your email administrator or email provider for further assistance if the error message persists.
  3. Click on Go to my account to use the in-product guide if you want to reevaluate your email setup and start over.

Why am I getting an error for the SPF record check? Can I send outgoing notifications without an SPF record? What does an SPF record do?

Zendesk does not require an SPF record to send outgoing notifications. The SPF record allows Zendesk to send emails from your domain. In the absence of an SPF record, the email shows as originating from Zendesk. If you receive an error for the SPF record check while forwarding functions correctly, make sure that there is an SPF record set up for that address.

articles.name: Why does the data in my report change over time?
articles.title: Why does the data in my report change over time?
articles.body:
Question

Why did my data change for dates in the past?

Each time I look at a report for a time period in the past, the data changes. For example, for the period of last month, the report showed a first reply time of 10 hours. A week later, the same report showed a first reply time of 9.7 hours. Why is this?

Answer

There are many reasons why past data may change over time:

  • If the data is sorted by the date refers to a solved ticket. When an older ticket finally gets solved, replied to, or given a satisfaction rating, that data will show up on past dates in the report. In addition, if you use the Solved tickets metric, as soon as one of those tickets switches back to pending or open, the ticket will no longer show in the report.
  • Data can also change when a solved ticket is re-opened. This impacts the number of solved tickets or full resolution time. For example, if a ticket is reopened and resolved during the time you last saw the report, the report will reflect the new time for solving that ticket.

  • Additionally, on a ticket, the first reply time target of SLAs does not start until the ticket gets a first public comment from an end user. Your first reply time average can change for this reason. For more information, see this article: Zendesk SLAs and first reply time.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why does past data change over time in Explore reports? Why did my analytics or reporting metrics change for past dates? Why does data for last month change in my report?

    Past data changes over time when you sort the report by the date that refers to a solved ticket, or when an older ticket receives a solution, a reply, or a CSAT or customer rating. Data also changes when a ticket switches from a solved state back to a pending or open ticket status, or when an agent reopens and resolves a ticket during the time you last saw the report. Additionally, the first reply time target of an SLA or deadline does not start until the ticket receives a first public comment from an end user or customer, which changes your first reply time average.

articles.name: Troubleshooting issues with your live chat connection
articles.title: Troubleshooting issues with your live chat connection
articles.body:
This troubleshooting guide includes steps to troubleshoot and resolve common chat connection errors. Use this guide if you're experiencing one or more of the following issues:

  • A gray X appear in my chat window with multiple different visitors
  • A disconnect error message, such as the following:
    • Disconnected, attempting to reconnect
    • Disconnected. Please refresh dashboard
  • Delays in the Chat dashboard functionality or the dashboard is unresponsive
  • Messages are duplicated due to disconnects
  • Chats are regularly unassigned even when you have chat routing configured
Note: Zendesk Chat is designed to automatically reconnect after a disconnection. Reconnections typically take a few seconds.

Follow the steps below to resolve issues with your live chat connection:

Step 1: Determine if there is an issue with your web browser

  1. Clear your cache and cookies. Clearing your browsing data can resolve formatting or loading issues.
  2. Log into your account in a private browsing window, incognito window, and different web browsers. If you have success in a different web browser or private browsing window, your issue is most likely due to a browser extension or setting.
  3. Remove extensions or restore default browser settings that may block your ability to chat with customers.
  4. Restart your web browser or computer. Issues with Chat can be caused by a local-storage issue, low browser memory, or an outdated browser.

For more information, see this article: Zendesk Support system requirements.

Step 2: Check for connection blockers

Security restrictions or network settings can sometimes cause trouble with your connection. Reach out to an admin or IT person to determine if any of the following restrictions apply to your account:

  1. Switch to a different network to see if you experience the same issue. Testing from a different network is the simplest way to identify if there is an issue with your local network.
  2. Ask your team if a VPN is being used. If so, do you experience your issue off of the VPN?
  3. Do you have a firewall or any anti-virus software blocking traffic to your site or ability to chat? If so, does the issue still occur when you temporarily disable these features? Read our recommendations for configuring your firewall for use with Zendesk and public IP addresses.

For more information, see the article: How to configure your network for Zendesk Chat

Step 3: Check for Visitor issues

If you are having trouble with specific visitors disconnecting with your agents, check for the following issues.

The web browser of visitors

An issue with the browser of a visitor can result in a visitor disappearing from the visitor list. Determine if the agent is experiencing chat issues with multiple visitors, or with a specific visitor, IP address, or company. Often this issue can be resolved by the end user. Ask the visitor to perform similar troubleshooting steps detailed in step one as their web browser or mobile device is most likely the source of the issue. For more information, see the article: Zendesk Chat system requirements.

A high volume of visitors

High traffic of visitors can be the cause of disconnects between visitors and agents. The high-load dashboard should be enabled to see if this feature alleviates the performance issues. For more information, see the article: Browsing your site's visitors for live chat.

Step 4: Check your chat connection settings

Review your chat connection settings to determine the issue.

View the following metrics in your chat connection settings:

  • Server: The Chat dashboard should connect to a server that is geographically closest to you.
  • Connection Type: If this metric displays another value other than "Ws" (Websocket) you could have a firewall blocking your connection.
  • Status: You should see the option “connected.”
  • Connection Quality: An indicator of the connection quality between your network and Zendesk Chat servers. The more filled in with black the line is, the better your connection. If the filled-in portion of the line is less than 50%, test your connection.
  • Connection Progress: The number "100" indicates that you are successfully connected and any value less than "100" indicates a connection issue.
  • Uptime: The amount of time (seconds/milliseconds) detected from your browser that Chat is connected over the total time of the session. If you were recently disconnected or have had breaks in the connection, the first number (time connected) will be lower than the second number (total session length).
  • D/C Count: If the disconnection count (D/C Count) is higher than the Connected Count then you are most likely experiencing an issue with your Chat dashboard connection.
  • Server Timeouts: Indicates the number of disconnections on the server end.

The settings previously mentioned help determine the possible source of your chat connection issues. Report these issues to an account admin or an internal IT Team to further troubleshoot once you have narrowed down the source of the issue.

Note: If necessary, you can reset your connection by clicking on the Force reconnect button. Force reconnecting will disconnect any active chats.

Step 5: Contact Zendesk Customer Support

If you still experience issues after you complete the steps in this guide, contact Zendesk Customer Support and provide the following information:

  • When did this issue first occur? How many times has it happened?
  • How many agents are impacted? Which agents and visitors are involved?
  • Determine if all of the agents on the same network (WiFi) or the same location (IP addresses).
  • Provide any relevant screenshots of the issue.

  • Send a screenshot of your Chat Connection settings, Chat Dashboard diagnostics, and support details immediately after the issue occurs.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why does a gray 'x' appear in my chat window? Why do I see a disconnect error message? Why is my inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard unresponsive? Why do chats regularly unassign?

    These issues indicate common live chat connection problems. Zendesk Chat automatically reconnects after a disconnection, which typically takes a few seconds. You can troubleshoot these issues by checking your web browser, network connection blockers, specific visitor browser issues, visitor traffic volume, and your chat connection settings.

    How do I clear browser issues blocking my chat? How do I fix an unresponsive inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard?

    You can determine if your web browser or inbox causes the connection issue. Follow these steps to resolve it:

    1. Clear your cache and cookies to resolve formatting or loading issues.
    2. Log into your account or instance in a private browsing window, incognito window, and different web browsers. Success in a different browser indicates an issue with a browser extension or setting.
    3. Remove extensions or restore default browser settings that block your ability to chat with customers or end users.
    4. Restart your web browser or computer to clear local-storage issues, low browser memory, or an outdated browser.

    How do I check for network connection blockers? Can a VPN block my chat connection? Can a firewall cause chat disconnects?

    Security restrictions or network settings cause trouble with your connection. Reach out to an admin, internal IT team, or IT person to check these restrictions:

    1. Switch to a different network to identify if there is an issue with your local network.
    2. Ask your team if a VPN is being used and test if the issue occurs off of the VPN.
    3. Determine if a firewall or anti-virus software blocks traffic to your site or your ability to chat by temporarily disabling these features.

    Why do specific visitors or customers keep disconnecting? Why do customers disappear from my visitor list?

    An issue with the web browser of a specific visitor, customer, or end user can cause them to disappear from the visitor list. Determine if the agent or assignee experiences issues with multiple visitors or with a specific visitor, IP address, or company or organization. Ask the visitor to clear their cache and cookies, use a private browsing window, remove extensions, or restart their web browser or mobile device.

    Can high traffic cause chat disconnects? How do I fix chat drops during high volume?

    High traffic or a high volume of visitors causes disconnects between visitors and agents or assignees. You can enable the high-load dashboard to see if this feature alleviates the performance issues.

    How do I check my chat connection settings? What does the connection quality line mean in chat diagnostics? What does Ws mean in chat connection type?

    Review the metrics in your chat connection settings to determine the source of the issue:

    • Server: The inbox or Agent Workspace dashboard must connect to a server geographically closest to you.
    • Connection Type: If this metric displays a value other than "Ws" (Websocket), a firewall might block your connection.
    • Status: You must see the option "connected."
    • Connection Quality: This line indicates the connection quality between your network and Zendesk Chat servers. Test your connection if the black filled-in portion of the line is less than 50%.
    • Connection Progress: The number "100" indicates a successful connection, while any value less than "100" indicates a connection issue.
    • Uptime: This metric tracks the time your browser connects over the total session length. Disconnections or breaks lower the first number.
    • D/C Count: If the disconnection count is higher than the Connected Count, you experience an issue with your dashboard connection.
    • Server Timeouts: This metric indicates the number of disconnections on the server end.

    Can I reset my chat connection? What happens if I click Force reconnect?

    Yes, you can reset your connection by clicking on the Force reconnect button. Clicking this button disconnects any active chats.

    How do I contact Zendesk Customer Support for chat connection issues? What information should I provide to help desk support for chat disconnects?

    If you complete the troubleshooting steps and still experience issues, contact Zendesk Customer Support. Provide the following details:

    1. The time the issue first occurred and its frequency.
    2. The number of impacted agents or assignees, and the specific agents and visitors involved.
    3. Whether all impacted agents are on the same network or location.
    4. Relevant screenshots of the issue, including a screenshot of your Chat Connection settings, Chat Dashboard diagnostics, and support details taken immediately after the issue occurs.

articles.name: What is the difference between the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets?
articles.title: What is the difference between the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets?
articles.body:
Question

What is the difference between these different Support datasets in Explore? In what situation is it better to use one dataset over the other?

Answer

While the Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets all allow you to report on ticket data, they approach that reporting from different perspectives.

  • The Tickets dataset provides data on current ticket information
  • The Updates history dataset provides data about actions taken on tickets throughout their lifetime
  • The Backlog dataset provides a snapshot of your ticket data at a specific time in the past. To learn more about this dataset, see Analyzing your ticket backlog history with Explore.

Find an example of how the different datasets work in this article: What is the difference between the solved tickets and tickets solved metrics in Explore?

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the difference between Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets? What is the difference between Support datasets in Explore or reporting or analytics? In what situation is it better to use one dataset over the other?

The Tickets, Updates history, and Backlog datasets all allow you to report on ticket data, but they approach that reporting from different perspectives. The Tickets dataset provides data on current ticket information. The Updates history dataset provides data about actions taken on tickets throughout their lifetime. The Backlog dataset provides a snapshot of your ticket data at a specific time in the past.

articles.name: When do chats time out?
articles.title: When do chats time out?
articles.body:
Question

What is the expected behavior for chat sessions to time out? When do idle conversations time out? Why can't I end a chat in messaging?

Answer

Live chat and messaging are different experiences in Zendesk. Live chat uses a real-time chat session that can end or time out, for example when a visitor leaves the website. Messaging is asynchronous and does not use a customer-facing session that the end user can end.

A conversation ends differently based on whether your account uses live chat or messaging. Click the tiles below to learn how to end chat or messaging conversations in different setups.

End a live chat session as an end user

In live chat, the visitor controls the chat session. The session remains active until the visitor ends it in one of these ways:

  • The visitor closes their browser window and the conversation times out
  • The visitor minimizes their chat with an agent and the conversation times out
  • The visitor remains idle on the website and the conversation times out

  • The visitor ends the chat by clicking the options menu icon and selecting End chat

In Chat Mobile SDKs, the chat conversation ends after any of these conditions:

  • No activity on the chat for one hour
  • The visitor is disconnected for one hour
  • The agent ends the chat, which resets the session disconnect timeout to five minutes
  • The visitor ends the chat, which immediately terminates the session
  • If push notifications are disabled, the session times out 30 minutes after the user backgrounds the app

For idle status details, see these notes:

  • If the visitor closes the browser with no windows open that contain the chat widget, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes
  • If a visitor using a mobile browser minimizes, switches tabs, or closes the browser containing the chat, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes
  • If the visitor idles on a website with the widget, the session stays open for 20 minutes before it ends automatically. An idle status means no mouse or keyboard input on the website for 10 minutes. The user does not need to interact with the widget, but must be actively working on the page where the widget loads. A variance of up to five minutes can occur depending on their connection to Chat.

Leave a live chat session as an agent

An agent can leave a live chat, but not end the session. The widget remains open for the end user until the end user ends the session. However, an agent can leave the chat without the session ending.

To leave a live chat session:

  1. Click the X button in the chat window

  2. Click End chat in the confirmation dialog

If an agent leaves a chat session, the end user can reopen the chat. In this situation, the agent will experience the following:

  • The chat returns to the agent's queue after an end user sends another chat message
  • The previous chat history is present for the agent because it is still the same chat conversation
  • Any subsequent messages are added to the chat transcript
  • After the live chat ends, all additional messages are added to the chat transcript history as a single entry
Remember: If an agent never joined the chat, that is a missed chat, and the conversation is moved into a ticket. A missed chat ticket is not created until the visitor ends the session.

For more information, see Why can my customers still chat after all agents have gone offline?

End a chat session in Agent Workspace

In Agent Workspace, end users can still end chats in all the ways listed above. The only difference in Agent Workspace is that the agent can end a chat in Agent Workspace with an end user with these steps:

  1. In Agent Workspace, click End chat

  2. In the confirmation dialog, click End chat

  3. The chat shows the visitor that the agent left the chat session

End a conversation in messaging

Unlike live chat, in messaging a session is not a customer-facing concept. The visitor cannot end a session. Instead, conversations move between active and inactive states.

  • A messaging conversation is Active when the end user has sent a message recently
  • A messaging conversation becomes Inactive after a specified time with no activity by the end user, or when the ticket status (or status category) changes to On-hold, Pending, or Solved. By default, Zendesk defines a message as inactive after 10 minutes with no response from the end user. Admins can modify the inactivity period.

The ticket's activity state, as well as the ticket status, affect routing, business rules, and agent capacity. For example, if you use omnichannel routing and have not turned on messaging activity routing, turn on automatic release capacity and set your inactivity period for messaging to help agents manage capacity more efficiently.

Messaging uses user presence to identify if end users are still on the website or have abandoned the conversation by closing the website. User presence helps prioritize active conversations over abandoned ones and increases agent utilization by releasing capacity when the end user abandons the conversation.

Messaging conversations can also end when an agent’s capacity is released. This closes the session automatically and prevents the end user from reopening it. The terminal ticket status can be set to On-hold, Pending, or left unchanged for flexible workflows.

Note: Omnichannel routing is enabled as the default routing experience for Zendesk Suite accounts created after December 5, 2024.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is live chat or messaging? How do live chat and messaging differ? What is the expected behavior for chat sessions to time out?

Live chat uses a real-time support conversation session that ends or times out when a customer or website visitor leaves your brand website. Messaging is asynchronous and does not use a customer-facing session that the customer or end user can end; instead, conversations move between active and inactive states based on customer activity or ticket progress status.

How do I end a live chat session as an end user? How can a customer or visitor close a real-time chat?

You can end a active live chat session as an end user or customer in several ways: close your browser window containing the chat widget, minimize the chat with an agent, remain idle on the website, or click the options menu icon and select End chat.

Can I end a chat in messaging? Why can't I close a messaging conversation?

No. In messaging, a session is not a customer-facing concept. The customer or visitor cannot end a session because messaging conversations move automatically between active and inactive states.

How do I leave a live chat session as an agent? How can a team member leave a real-time chat?

You can leave a live chat session as an agent or support person by following these steps:

  1. Click the X button in the chat window.
  2. Click End chat in the confirmation dialog box.
The chat session remains open for the end user, and it returns to your queue if the customer sends another message.

 

How do I end a chat session in Agent Workspace? How can an agent or team member close a chat in the dashboard inbox?

You can end a live chat session completely as an agent within the Agent Workspace dashboard by following these steps:

  1. In the Agent Workspace inbox, click End chat.
  2. In the confirmation dialog box, click End chat.
The conversation then shows the visitor or customer that the team member left the chat session.

 

What are the session timeouts for Chat Mobile SDKs? When does a mobile app chat session expire?

A mobile SDK support conversation terminates immediately if the customer ends the chat, or it times out five minutes after an agent ends the chat. The session also closes automatically after one hour of no activity or one hour of customer disconnection. If you disable push notifications, the session times out 30 minutes after the user backgrounds the app.

What are the idle status timeout limits for web browser live chat? When do inactive web visitor chats close?

If a visitor closes their browser window or minimizes/switches tabs on a mobile browser, the session times out within 20 seconds to two minutes. If a visitor idles on a website with the widget, the session stays open for 20 minutes. Idle status means your customer provides no mouse or keyboard input on the web page for 10 minutes, with an allowed connection variance of up to five minutes.

When does a messaging conversation become inactive? What is the default messaging inactivity period?

By default, Zendesk defines a messaging conversation as inactive after 10 minutes with no response or activity from the end user. It also becomes inactive when the ticket progress status category changes to On-hold, Pending, or Solved. Admins can modify this inactivity period to help manage agent capacity.

articles.name: How do I verify that agents who receive “Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you” have the correct permissions?
articles.title: How do I verify that agents who receive “Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you” have the correct permissions?
articles.body:
Question

How do I ensure my agents have the correct permissions in live chat? Why do I get the message Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you when you launch the Chat dashboard? How do I sign in?

Answer

This message appears when Chat is turned off for the agent's profile. An admin manages which agents access Chat through Admin Center. Contact your team if you aren't an admin.

To turn on Chat on your own profile:

  1. From the Support dashboard, click your Avatar in the top-right corner and select View profile
  2. In the left panel, next to Role, click Manage in Admin Center
  3. Click the checkbox under Access for Chat. Select Agent or Admin as needed.
  4. Click Save
Note: When the only Chat seat is turned off, another admin from Support needs to turn on Chat in your profile. If no other admin or license exists, contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

To turn on Chat for another agent:

  1. In Admin Center, click 
    People in the sidebar, then select Team > Team members
  2. From the list, click the agent you want to update
  3. On the Roles and access tab of the agent's page, click Enabled next to Chat
  4. Update the user's role to Agent or Admin as needed

  5. Click Save at the bottom of the page 

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    Why am I getting the message "Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you" when launching the Chat dashboard? How can I sign in? How do I verify that agents who receive "Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you" have the correct permissions or access level? How can I ensure my agents or my team have the correct permissions in live chat?

    This message appears when Chat is disabled for the agent profile or support person profile. An admin manages which agents or our users can access Chat through the Admin Center. Contact your team or my team if you are not an admin.

    How do I enable Chat on my own profile? How can I turn on live chat permissions for my account?

    To enable Chat on your own profile, use these steps:

    1. From the Support dashboard or help desk inbox, click your Avatar in the top-right corner and select View profile.
    2. In the left panel, next to Role, click Manage in Admin Center.
    3. Click the checkbox under Access for Chat. Select Agent or Admin as needed.
    4. Click Save.

    How do I enable Chat for another agent or support person? How can I grant live chat access level to my team member?

    To enable Chat for another agent or team member, follow these steps:

    1. In Admin Center, click People in the sidebar, then select Team Team members.
    2. From the list, click the agent or our user you want to update.
    3. On the Roles and access tab of the agent's page, click Enabled next to Chat.
    4. Update the user's role to Agent or Admin as needed.
    5. Click Save at the bottom of the page.

    Can I enable Chat on my profile if the only Chat seat or subscription is disabled? What happens if no other admin or license exists?

    When the only Chat seat or subscription is disabled, another admin from Support or help desk needs to enable Chat in your profile. If no other admin or license exists, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

articles.name: How do I export my data and reports from all Zendesk products?
articles.title: How do I export my data and reports from all Zendesk products?
articles.body:
Answer

Exports are available for each product. Select an option from the list to learn how to export data from the product you use.

Understand export permissions

Export capabilities vary by role and product.

Support tickets, users, and organizations

Guide knowledge base

Note: The Guide knowledge base has no CSV export option. You must use the help center API.

Messaging and legacy live chat

How to check your CSV export permission

To check your CSV export permission:

  • Go to Admin Center People Team Team members
  • Find your name and click to open your profile
  • Look at the Role field
  • If you have a custom role, click the role name to see your permissions
  • Look for CSV Export in the permissions list

To do a quick test: Go to a view with tickets and look for the Export button at the top-right. If it is present, you have the CSV Export permission. If it is missing, you do not have it.

Export data from Support

To export data directly from Support, see Export ticket, user, or organization data from your account.

Verify your plan against these requirements. If your plan allows exports, yet you don't see the Reports option, Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

For more information, see:

  • How to export a list of users
  • What is the recommended method to regularly export Zendesk Support data?
  • What are my ticket export options?

  • Where is the data export option?

    Note: The ability to export data to a JSON, CSV, or XML file is available to admins and agents with a custom role that has export permissions.

Export reports from Analytics

To export a dashboard, see Export a dashboard tab.

Note: You can only export one tab at a time.

To export a report, see Work with reports from within a report.

Export data from Sales

To export data from Sales, see Export data from Zendesk Sales.

Export data from Knowledge

Important: There is no CSV export button for Knowledge content.

Available export methods

Method 1: Help center API article-by-article

This is best for complete data backups and migrations to another platform.

To export these items:

  • Article content for all locales
  • Article metadata such as authors, dates, and votes
  • Categories and sections structure
  • Article attachments that you must download separately

Limitations include the following:

  • Requires API knowledge or scripts
  • Must export articles through pagination
  • Time-consuming for large knowledge bases of more than 100 articles

For full documentation, see the help center API.

Method 2: Web scraping is not recommended

Web scraping uses tools to copy published articles from your public help center.

This is not recommended because of the following reasons:

  • Only gets published content and loses drafts and internal articles
  • Loses metadata such as author, creation date, votes, and comments
  • Breaks formatting often
  • Doesn't get article relationships or structure
  • Against the Zendesk Terms of Service if automated at scale

Method 3: Professional services

This is best for large-scale migrations and complex data transformations.

Zendesk professional services provide the following:

  • Complete knowledge base export
  • Preserves all metadata and relationships
  • Migration to another Zendesk instance or external platform
  • Data transformation and cleanup

Note: This is a paid service, so contact your account representative for a consultation.

Method 4: Community scripts

Community scripts are community-contributed export scripts found on the Zendesk Developer Community or GitHub.

Use this method with caution for the following reasons:

  • Community tools are not officially supported by Zendesk
  • May break if the API changes
  • Test thoroughly and review the code for security before you run the script

Quick comparison

Recommendation: For serious backup and migration needs, use the help center API or professional services.

Export data from Messaging or legacy live chat

Important: First, determine which product you have.

How to check if you use messaging or legacy live chat

  • Go to Admin Center > Channels. If you see Messaging and social, you have messaging. If you only see Chat, you have legacy live chat.
  • In messaging, conversations persist across sessions. In legacy live chat, sessions end when the customer closes the window.
  • Messaging is available on Suite plans such as Professional and Enterprise. Legacy live chat is a legacy product.

Messaging and Sunshine Conversations export

To export the data:

  • Go to Admin Center Channels Messaging and social Messaging
  • Click your messaging channel
  • Go to the Analytics or Export section to export conversation data. Location varies by setup.

You get conversation transcripts, metadata such as customer information, timestamps, and agent assigned, and message content. Alternatively, use the Sunshine Conversations API for a programmatic export.

Legacy live chat export

You can export your past chats from the History page in the legacy live chat dashboard.

To export the data:

  • Go to Admin Center Channels Chat Dashboard
  • Click Analytics
  • Export chat data as a CSV or scheduled email reports

Limitations include historical data retention that varies by plan and might not include very old chats. Alternatively, use the live chat API for a programmatic export.

For information on how to export conversion data, see Export Chat Conversion Tracking data.

Additional export considerations

Customer list add-on

To export end-user lists, some accounts have the customer list add-on which provides the following:

  • Enhanced user export capabilities
  • Scheduled user exports
  • User segment exports

This is not available on all plans. Contact your account representative to check eligibility and pricing.

To check if you have it: Go to Admin Center People Customers. Look for enhanced export options. If you only see a basic user export, you do not have the add-on.

Export frequency limits

Be aware of rate limits when you export data:

  • UI exports and CSV downloads: There is no strict limit on frequency, but very large exports of more than 100,000 records might time out. Split large exports by date range if needed.
  • API exports: Rate limits apply. Incremental exports are recommended for large datasets.

  • Scheduled exports: You can schedule daily and weekly exports for some data types to be emailed to specified addresses. This is automatic and does not count against manual export usage.

    THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

    How do I check my CSV export permission? Can I check my data download rights? How do I find if I can export data?

    Scope and limitations

    To check your permissions, navigate to Admin Center People Team Team members. Select your name to open your profile and check the Role field. For custom roles, select the role name to view your permissions list and look for CSV Export. Alternatively, view a ticket list and look for the Export button at the top-right.

    Can I export Guide knowledge base articles to CSV? Can I download my help articles as a CSV? Is CSV export available for help center content?

    Scope and limitations

    No. The Guide knowledge base or help articles platform has no CSV export option. You must use the help center API to download this data.

    How do I export my Guide knowledge base? What is the best method to backup help articles? How can I migrate my help center content?

    Concept

    You can export your Guide knowledge base using the help center API article-by-article. This method serves best for complete data backups and platform migrations. It extracts article content for all locales, metadata like authors, dates, and votes, categories and sections structure, and article attachments which you download separately.

    Scope and limitations

    The help center API method requires API knowledge or scripts. You must export articles through pagination, which becomes time-consuming for large knowledge bases of more than 100 articles.

    Can I use web scraping to export help articles? Is web scraping recommended for Guide backups?

    Pro tip

    Web scraping is not recommended to copy published articles from your public help center. It loses drafts, internal articles, and metadata like author, creation date, votes, and comments. It often breaks formatting, fails to capture article relationships or structure, and violates the Zendesk Terms of Service if automated at scale.

    What do Zendesk professional services provide for data migration? Can professional services export my knowledge base?

    Concept

    Zendesk professional services provide complete knowledge base exports while preserving all metadata and relationships. They handle data transformation, cleanup, and migration to another Zendesk instance or external platform. This paid service works best for large-scale migrations and complex data transformations.

    Can I use community scripts to export my help center? Are community backup tools safe to use?

    Pro tip

    Use community-contributed export scripts from the Zendesk Developer Community or GitHub with caution. Zendesk does not officially support these tools, and they may break if the API changes. Test them thoroughly and review the code for security before you run the script.

    How do I check if I use messaging or legacy live chat? Am I using messaging or legacy chat? How can I verify my chat product type?

    Scope and limitations

    Navigate to Admin Center Channels. If you see Messaging and social, you use messaging. If you only see Chat, you use legacy live chat. In messaging, conversations persist across sessions, and the feature comes with Suite plans like Professional and Enterprise. In legacy live chat, sessions end when the customer closes the window.

    How do I export data from messaging or Sunshine Conversations? How do I download chat transcripts and conversation history?

    Scope and limitations

    Navigate to Admin Center Channels Messaging and social Messaging and select your messaging channel. Go to the Analytics or Export section to export conversation data. This download includes conversation transcripts, metadata like customer information, timestamps, assigned agents, and message content. Alternatively, use the Sunshine Conversations API for programmatic exports.

    How do I export data from legacy live chat? How do I download history from legacy chat?

    Scope and limitations

    Export past chats from the History page in the legacy live chat dashboard. Navigate to Admin Center Channels Chat Dashboard, select Analytics, and export chat data as a CSV or scheduled email reports. Historical data retention varies by plan and might exclude very old chats. Alternatively, use the live chat API for a programmatic export.

    What is the customer list add-on? Can I schedule user list exports? What are enhanced user export capabilities?

    Concept

    The customer list add-on or paid plugin provides enhanced user export capabilities, scheduled user exports, and user segment exports to download end-user lists. This feature is not available on all plans. Navigate to Admin Center People Customers to check for enhanced export options; a basic user export indicates you do not have the add-on.

    What are the export frequency limits? Will my large data export time out? Can I schedule automatic reports?

    Scope and limitations

    UI exports and CSV downloads have no strict frequency limits, but exports exceeding 100,000 records might time out. Split large downloads by date range if needed. API exports have rate limits and require incremental exports for large datasets. You can schedule daily and weekly automatic exports via email for certain data types without affecting manual export usage limits.

    How do I export data from Support? How do I download ticket, user, or organization data?

    Scope and limitations

    You can export ticket, user, or organization data directly from Support or your help desk system. The capability to export data to a JSON, CSV, or XML file is available to admins and agents with a custom role that has export permissions. Verify your pricing tier requirements if the option is missing, or contact Zendesk Customer Support.

    How do I export reports from Explore analytics? Can I download a dashboard?

    Scope and limitations

    To export a dashboard tab from Explore or your reporting tool, navigate to the specific tab. You can only export one tab at a time. To download an individual report, utilize the options within that specific report.

    How do I export data from Sales? How do I download sales data?

    Scope and limitations

    To export data from Sales, utilize the specific export functions within the Zendesk Sales product interface.

articles.name: Can Zendesk Support equally distribute tickets amongst agents or groups?
articles.title: Can Zendesk Support equally distribute tickets amongst agents or groups?
articles.body:
Question

Is there an option to equally assign incoming tickets to agents automatically?

Answer

Yes, turn on and configure omnichannel routing to automatically distribute tickets among your agents.

Omnichannel routing assigns tickets based on agent availability, capacity, percentages, and time elapsed since being assigned a ticket.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Can Zendesk Support or help desk equally distribute tickets amongst agents? Can I automatically assign incoming tickets to my team or department equally? Is there an option to equally assign incoming tickets to support persons automatically?

Turn on and configure omnichannel routing to automatically distribute tickets among your agents or support persons. Omnichannel routing assigns tickets based on agent availability, capacity, percentages, and time elapsed since being assigned a ticket.
